title: Method of recycling culture media from organic carbon supplied microalgae cultures
abstract: Methods of recycling culture media in microalgae cultures are described herein. Aspects of the method include centrifugation, filtration, and ozonation to reduce the concentration of bacteria in the culture medium. The method can surprisingly be performed with inclusion of one or more substances that are reduced by the ozone treatment, such as microalgae cultureinhibiting compounds and/or substances that are surprisingly not impacted by ozonation.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: a. Centrifuging a microalgae culture comprising culture media, bacteria, and a population of microalgae cells, to separate the microalgae cells from a centrate comprising a first concentration of bacteria (BC) and culture media, the culture media comprising a first malic acid concentration (MAC) and/or a first hexose concentration (HC); b. Filtering the centrate; and c. Treating the filtered centrate with ozone for an effective amount of time to produce a treated medium characterized in having a second concentration of bacteria lower than the first concentration of bacteria and (a) a second MAC that is lower than the first MAC, (b) a second HC that is substantially the same as the first HC, or (c) a second MAC that is lower than the first MAC and a second HC that is substantially the same as the first HC, 2. The method of claim I, wherein the culture comprises a first acetic acid concentration (AAC) in the medium before ozone treatment and the second AAC in the treated medium is substantially the same as the first AAC, 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the hexose is glucose. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, further comprising inoculating the microalgae cells in a first subsequent culture with a first recycled culture medium comprising at least 50% of the treated medium. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the effective amount of time the filtered centrate is treated with ozone is at least 1 hour. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the effective amount of time the filtered centrate is treated with ozone for up to 6 hours. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the second concentration of bacteria is at least two orders of magnitude lower than the first concentration. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the medium comprises malic acid and the second MAC is at least 67% less than the first MAC. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, further comprising repeating the steps of centrifuging, filtering, and treating with ozone the filtered centrate from the first subsequent culture, and inoculating microalgae cells in a second subsequent culture with a second recycled culture medium comprising at least 25% of the treated medium. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising repeating the steps of centrifuging, filtering, and treating the filtered centrate with ozone from the second subsequent culture, and inoculating microalgae cells in a third subsequent culture with a third recycled culture medium comprising at least 25% of the treated medium. 1 1. The method of claim 9, further comprising repeating the steps of centrifuging, filtering, and treating the filtered centrate from a subsequent culture with ozone, and inoculating microalgae cells in a further subsequent culture with a recycled culture medium comprising at least 25% of the treated medium up to five times without reducing the microalgae growth rate in dry weight grams per liter per day more than 20% as compared to a control culture of microalgae with 100% newly prepared culture media that has not be treated or recycled. 12. The method of claim 9, wherein the microalgae cells are inoculated in a culture comprising at least 50% of the treated medium. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the first recycled culture medium comprises at least 75% of the treated medium. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the first recycled culture medium comprises 100% of the treated medium. 15. The method of any one of claims .1 -14, wherein the centrate is filtered with a membrane filter, w herein the membrane filter has a pore size of 0.1-0.5 microns. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherem an oxidation reduction potential of the centrate is maintained in the range of 800-900 mV during treatment with ozone. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the filtered centrate is treated with an amount of ozone in the range of 55 to 111 mg of ozone per liter of centrate. 18. The method of any one of claims 1 -17, wherein the microalgae comprises microalgae of the genus selected from Chlorella, Schizochyirium, and Scenedesmus. 19. A composition comprising: a. A population of microalgae cells: and b. Culture media comprising: i. At least one of acetic acid and acetate; and 

 ii. At least 25% recycled culture media from a previous microalgae culture, wherein the recycled culture media has been filtered and treated with ozone. 20. A method of reducing the addition of fresh media and nutrients to a non-axenic culture of Chiorella: a. Providing a culture of microalgae comprising a supply of at least one of acetate or acetic acid as an organic carbon source, a population of Chiorella cells, and at least some bacteria in a culture medium comprising water, nutrients, malic acid, and acetic acid; b. Centrifuging the culture to separate the Chiorella cells from a centrate comprising a first concentration of bacteria and centnfuged culture media, wherein the centrifuged culture media comprises a first concentration of malic acid and a fi rst concentration of acetic acid; c. Filtering the centrate to form a filtered centrate; d. Treating the filtered centrate with ozone for an effective amount of time to produce a treated medium with a second concentration of malic acid lower than the first concentration of malic acid, a second concentration of bacteria lower than the first concentration of bacteria, and a concentration of acetic acid substantially the same as the first concentration of acetic acid; e. Inoculating Chiorella cells in a first subsequent culture supplied with at least one of acetate or acetic acid with a first recycled culture medium comprising at least 25% of the treated medium and the remainder comprising of freshly prepared culture media; and f. Supplying less of at least one of acetate and acetic acid to the first subsequent culture than a control culture supplied with at least one of acetate or acetic acid would receive which comprises 100% freshly prepared culture media.
